content.text: {"overview":"This A-major fugue in compound triple meter (9/8) unfolds across 54 bars with textbook three-voice contrapuntal architecture, tracing a systematic circle-of-fifths journey through relative minor regions—e minor, b minor, f# minor, D major—before returning emphatically to the tonic; its most distinctive feature is the stretto section in bars 25–35, where the subject overlaps in close imitation across all three voices, generating intense contrapuntal density before the virtuosic sequential episode drives through f# and c# minor toward a plagal-tinged final cadence.","cross_piece_tags":["fugue","counterpoint","circle_of_fifths","ternary","dramatic","pedal_point","modal_mixture","melancholic"]}
